data.title: Chow Tai Fook hits nearly 2-year high on H2 profit beat
data.description:
** Chow Tai Fook Jewellery Group jumps 11.2% to HK$13.66, highest since August 2023

** Stock set for biggest one-day pct rise since September 26, 2024

** HK-based co's net profit for year ended March 31 falls 9% y/y; revenue slides 17.5% y/y; gross profit margin expands to 29.5% from year-ago 24%

** Jefferies maintains "buy", lifts PT to HK$14 from HK$10.4, citing co's H2 FY25 net profit, which jumped 74% y/y and beat market estimates thanks to lower-than-expected opex ratio

** UBS says FY25 operating profit beat, co's FY26 outlook better than market expected

** Stock nearly doubles YTD

data.title: Taiwan's Bureau Of Labor Fund Awards $1.6 Bln Mandates
data.description:
TAIWAN'S BUREAU OF LABOR FUND:

  • AWARDS $1.6 BILLION MANDATES OF GLOBAL CLIMATE TRANSITION PASSIVE STOCKS

  • AWARDS MANDATES TO BLACKROCK FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT, HSBC GLOBAL ASSET MANAGEMENT (UK), STATE STREET GLOBAL ADVISORS SINGAPORE LTD, UBS ASSET MANAGEMENT (SINGAPORE) LTD

data.title: PowerChina Bags Wind Power Bid for 11 Billion Yuan
data.description:
A subsidiary of Power Construction Corp. of China or PowerChina won the bid for the construction of a wind power project for 10.8 billion yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Friday.

China Power Construction Group Central South Survey and Design Institute won the bid for the Huadian Donggang Offshore Wind Power Project in Dandong, Liaoning Province, China.

The project's phase I will have a capacity of 1,000 megawatts.

data.title: S-Oil Shares Rise 8.3% to KRW64,100 Amid Middle East Tension
TRENDE Announces Strategic Capital and Business Alliance with Tokyu Land
data.description:
TOKYO, June 13, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - TRENDE Inc., a leading renewable energy solutions provider in Japan, today announced the formation of a strategic capital and business alliance with Tokyu Land Corporation, a leading Japanese real estate developer. This partnership marks a significant milestone in advancing the adoption of distributed renewable energy and peer-to-peer (P2P) electricity trading in Japan's real estate sector.

As part of the extension round of TRENDE's Series C financing executed on March 19, 2025, Tokyu Land Corporation has joined as a new shareholder. This investment underscores Tokyu Land's commitment to accelerating the deployment of sustainable energy solutions across its extensive real estate portfolio.

Driving Innovation in Real Estate and Energy

Under the new alliance, TRENDE and Tokyu Land will collaborate on several key initiatives, including:

- Strategic operation of power generation businesses utilizing residential solar and battery systems to provide enhanced grid flexibility and resilience.

- Joint development and social implementation of P2P electricity trading platforms that enable property owners and residents to directly transact renewable energy, thereby increasing real estate value and promoting energy self-sufficiency.

- Leveraging Tokyu Land's "ReENE" renewable energy brand and real estate expertise with TRENDE's advanced technology and market-leading P2P platform to deliver innovative, customer-centric energy solutions.

Tadatoshi Senoo, President of TRENDE Inc., commented: "This partnership with Tokyu Land Corporation is a pivotal step toward realizing our mission of building a society that actively utilizes renewable energy. By combining our strengths in technology and real estate, we aim to accelerate the expansion of P2P electricity trading and unlock new value for communities and property owners alike."

About TRENDE Inc.

TRENDE Inc. provides customer-centric renewable energy solutions in Japan through its solar and battery leasing service "Teraris" (https://teraris.jp/) and innovative peer-to-peer (P2P) electricity trading platform. With a mission to accelerate renewable energy adoption and redefine Japan's energy ecosystem, TRENDE is backed by prominent investors including Itochu Corporation, Idemitsu Kosan Co., Ltd., Dubai Electricity and Water Authority (DEWA), Tokyo Century Corporation, National Federation of Agricultural Cooperative Associations (ZEN-NOH), Zen-Noh Energy Corporation, Toshiba Corporation and Tokyu Land Corporation. For more information, visit https://trende.jp/.

About Tokyu Land Corporation

Headquartered in Shibuya, Tokyo, Tokyu Land Corporation is a leading real estate developer committed to creating sustainable communities. The company operates the "ReENE" brand for renewable energy initiatives. For more details, visit https://www.tokyu-land.co.jp/ and https://tokyu-reene.com/.

Media Contact:

TRENDE Inc.

Public Relations

Email: pr@trende.jp

Source: TRENDE

Copyright 2025 JCN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Oil Prices Surge After Israel Attack on Iran
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

EU industrial production, foreign trade; Germany CPI; no major corporate trading updates expected

Opening Call:

European stock futures fell early Friday. Asian stock benchmarks weakened; the dollar rose; Treasury yields fell; while oil futures surged and gold strengthened.

Equities:

European and U.S. stock futures were lower after Israel launched a wide-ranging attack on Iran's nuclear program and military leadership overnight, killing the head of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and striking dozens of targets in an operation that pushes the region into a new conflict with uncertain consequences.

The attack rattled markets and sent investors fleeing to safe-haven assets. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed that a military operation against Iran had begun and would last "as many days as it takes."

The strikes came after Iran said Thursday that it would soon open a third uranium-enrichment site, a major escalation after the International Atomic Energy Agency determined for the first time in two decades that Tehran had failed to comply with its non-proliferation obligations.

Forex:

Risky assets could pull back amid rising geopolitical risks in the Middle East, DBS strategist Chang Wei Liang said. "Markets will carefully assess risks of an escalation." Safe haven assets such as the Swiss franc and Japanese yen rallied against the dollar, while other Asian currencies lost ground.

Bonds:

The 10-year yield on U.S. Treasurys fell, after the attack on Iranian nuclear facilities spurred investors to pare down risk.

-

The risk-reward balance in Treasury markets is "starting to look attractive," Harris Oakmark's Adam Abbas said. "We're getting to a point where there's sufficient premium baked into those instruments that investors who are buy-and-hold and long-term and disciplined can start wanting to get exposure."

Energy:

Crude oil prices surged after Israel attacked Iran's nuclear facilities. The worsening conflict in the Middle East raises the risk of not just oil supply disruptions from key producing nations, but also of contagion in other neighboring oil producers, Phillip Nova's Priyanka Sachdeva said.

ING's Warren Patterson said that the oil market has to price in a larger risk premium for any potential supply disruptions after the attack.

Metals:

Gold extended gains after Israel attacked Iran's nuclear facilities. "Risk-off sentiment prevailed in the global markets due to the incident, " said Tina Teng, an independent market analyst.

-

Copper edged lower in Asian trading. The base metal's prices are unlikely to rise further in the short term, as moderate U.S. PPI data reinforced expectations for two Fed rate cuts this year, Nanhua Futures analysts said.

-

Iron ore prices were down amid soft Chinese demand. Demand for steel from infrastructure could drop 7% this year given low orders from contractors and persistent demand weakness from the property sector, Citi analysts said. However, positive sentiment from potentially strong orders late in the second half of the year provides tailwinds for iron ore and steel prices, they said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Israel Launches Attack on Iran's Nuclear Facilities

Israel launched a wide-ranging attack on Iran's nuclear program and military leadership overnight, killing the head of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps and striking dozens of targets in an operation that pushes the region into a new conflict with uncertain consequences.

Israel said the attack targeted Iran's nuclear program and other military sites, as well as Iranian military commanders and nuclear scientists. Dozens of planes wrapped up the first wave of the attack before dawn Friday, Israel said.

This crucial corner of the energy market is at high risk from an all-out war between Israel and Iran - and it's not oil

All eyes are on oil markets and the key Strait of Hormuz, the markets' crossroads, but an all-out war between Israel and Iran would pose a serious threat to eastern Mediterranean natural-gas fields - and risk spilling into Europe's liquefied natural-gas supply.

Israel late Thursday said it attacked sites in Iran, raising fresh fears of a widespread conflict in the Middle East and sending oil futures higher and U.S. stock futures lower.

The Case for Rate Cuts Is Growing

President Trump's tariffs present the Federal Reserve with two conflicting challenges. First, they raise prices, which weakens the case for cutting interest rates. Second, they sap confidence and demand, which strengthens the case.

To date, the Fed has focused on the first risk, keeping its interest rate target between 4.25% and 4.5% since December. It might soon have to pivot to the second. Evidence is accumulating that inflation, despite tariffs, has been milder than feared, while the labor market might be deteriorating.

Scale AI Gets Meta Investment That Values It at More Than $29 Billion

Scale AI has been valued at more than $29 billion after a significant investment from Meta Platforms that will see its founder, Alexandr Wang, join the social-media giant to work on its AI efforts.

The artificial-intelligence startup announced the investment on Thursday, confirming an earlier report by The Wall Street Journal.

Adobe Keeps Pivoting as AI Transforms Customer Needs

Adobe is making big changes to keep up with artificial intelligence, but investors aren't sold.

Chief Financial Officer Dan Durn pinpointed AI developments as the main driver behind the software company's higher-than-expected revenue in the fiscal second quarter, which allowed management to raise its full-year outlook.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: New China Life Insurance to Invest 11 Billion Yuan Into 23 Billion Yuan Equity Fund
data.description:
New China Life Insurance plans to invest for half of the units of a 22.5 billion yuan private equity fund, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing on Thursday.

Guofeng Xinghua Honghu Zhiyuan No. 1 Private Securities Investment Fund III will invest into eligible large listed companies which are constituents of the CSI A500 Index.

The insurer will invest 11.25 billion yuan in the fund. China Life Insurance will also invest the same amount, the filing said.

New China Life Insurance's Hong Kong shares rose 1% and Shanghai shares slipped 1%.

data.title: Energy Shares Rise After Attack on Iran
data.description:
By Peter Landers

Energy shares gained ground in Asia after Israel's attack on Iran, in line with the rise in oil prices, while movements in other markets were relatively restrained as investors weighed the potential fallout. Japan's Nikkei average was down 1.2% in afternoon trading Friday, and other Asian markets were down slightly. The dollar and other safe-haven currencies rose.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: ASX's Cost Outlook Keeps Creeping Higher — Market Talk
data.description: The cost implications of ASX's five-year strategy are gradually becoming clearer to its bears at UBS. The Australian market operator is two years into the strategy and its cost outlook is continuing to creep higher, UBS analysts tell clients in a note. They say that the company's critical technology transformation appears to be on track, but see depreciation and amortization costs rapidly rising as ASX progresses its multi-year capital expenditure catch-up. The prospects of offsetting this through revenue gains are unclear, they add. UBS trims its target price by 0.6% to A$69.10 and keeps a sell rating on the stock. Shares are up 1.0% at A$72.69. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Indonesia Retail Sales to Continue Growth in May
data.description:
Retail sales in Indonesia are expected to continue to grow in May, according to a Bank Indonesia survey released Friday.

The real sales index is expected to rise 2.6% annually to a level of 234 in May, driven by increases seen in cultural and recreational goods, food, beverages and tobacco, as well as clothing.

The real sales index is projected to edge down by only 0.6% on a monthly basis as higher sales are expected due to higher demand during the national religious holidays commemorating Vesak and the Ascension of Jesus Christ, the central bank said.

data.title: Chinese firms in talks to join group to buy Li Ka-shing's ports, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Chinese ports operator China Cosco Shipping Corporation is among the firms in talks to participate in a global consortium seeking to buy 43 ports from billionaire Li Ka-shing-owned CK Hutchison , Bloomberg News reported on Thursday.

CK Hutchison said last month that Mediterranean Shipping Company, run by the family of Italian billionaire Gianluigi Aponte, was the main investor in a group seeking the portfolio of ports, which includes two near the Panama Canal, for $22.8 billion.

Negotiation for the deal that covers 43 ports in 23 countries is on an exclusive basis between CK Hutchison and the consortium for 145 days until July 27.

Adding Chinese investors to the consortium emerged as one of the options after high-level talks in Switzerland last month between Chinese and U.S. officials, the Bloomberg report said.

China Cosco Shipping Corp is among the state-backed companies in talks with the consortium run by the Aponte family, the report said.

CK Hutchison and China Cosco Shipping didn't immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment. Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

The deal has been through weeks of global scrutiny and criticism in China and the U.S. over CK Hutchison's plan to sell the ports to a consortium, previously led by U.S. investment firm BlackRock . BlackRock remains part of the group.

data.title: United Food's Attributable Loss Widens in Fiscal Q4
data.description:
United Food's attributable loss to owners of the company in fiscal Q4 widened to 83.0 million yuan from 1.8 million yuan a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

Loss per share widened to 0.367 yuan compared with 0.004 yuan in the comparable period.

The company did not report any revenue from continued operations during the period or in the comparable period.

data.title: Stocks in India Hit 5-week Low
data.description:
SENSEX decreased to a 5-week low of 80428.00 Index Points.

Over the past 4 weeks, SENSEX gained 0.67%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 6.35%.

data.title: CHC Healthcare Sets Conversion Price, Premium Rate of Bonds
data.description:
CHC Healthcare Group set the conversion price and premium rate for its fifth domestic issue of unsecured convertible corporate bonds.

The company fixed the conversion price at NT$48.4 per share with a premium rate of 105.2%, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Eni Unit Opens New Logistics Base in Congo
data.description:
Italian energy group Eni's (ENI.MI) Congo-based unit inaugurated a logistics platform at the Saipem base to support its liquefied natural gas project and other infrastructure developments in the country.

Eni Congo's 190,000 square-meter Yasika logistics base has a quay, jetty, office space, and mud and cement treatment units for drilling activities, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on June 13
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Friday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

POLITICS

Italy's Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani on Thursday said his country needs at least 10 years to raise defence spending and comply with new targets due to be agreed by NATO, adding that a deal on them could soon be reached.

ECONOMY

ISTAT releases April foreign trade data (0800 GMT).

The Italian government on Thursday earmarked 200 million euros to keep afloat the steel company Acciaierie d'Italia (ADI), formerly known as Ilva, while negotiations to sell it are ongoing, the industry minister said.

COMPANIES

STELLANTIS

U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday warned he may soon hike auto tariffs, arguing that could prod automakers to speed U.S. investments.

PIRELLI

The tyremaker investors on Thursday approved the group's earnings report for last year despite the opposition of its largest shareholder, the Chinese state-controlled group Sinochem , the company said.

GENERALI , MEDIOBANCA , BANCA GENERALI

The insurer said on Thursday it had begun evaluating Mediobanca's bid for Generali's unit Banca Generali and the potential subsequent partnership with Mediobanca in the wealth management sector.

ENI

The energy group is closely monitoring the security situation in Iraq, a spokesperson said on Thursday after the United States decided to evacuate its embassy in the country.

ENERGY

Italiana Petroli, which is currently in talks with three suitors interested in buying the oil refiner, is expected to take a decision on the potential 3 billion euro deal at the end of june, two sources said on Thursday.

DIARY

Rome, Bank of Italy holds first 2025 Macroprudential Policies Committee meeting, chaired by Bank of Italy Governor Fabio Panetta, with market regulator CONSOB Chairman Paolo Savona, insurance supervisor IVASS President Luigi Federico Signorini, Treasury Director General Riccardo Barbieri (1400 GMT).

Rapallo, business lobby Confindustria's Youth Group starts its annual meeting (1230 GMT); ends on June 14. On Friday expected attendees include Infrastructure and Transport Minister Matteo Salvini, Industry Minister Adolfo Urso, Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani.

data.title: Global nuclear arms spending up 11% in 2024, campaign group says
data.description:
Spending on nuclear weapons by the world's nine nuclear-armed nations rose by 11% in 2024, a report by the International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons said on Friday.

The $10 billion annual increase to $100.2 billion went towards modernising and in some cases expanding nuclear arsenals, according to ICAN, a global civil society coalition that seeks the total elimination of atomic weapons.

"Nuclear-armed countries could have paid the United Nations' budget 28 times with what they spent to build and maintain nuclear weapons in 2024," the report said.

The U.S. recorded the largest annual increase in nuclear spending in 2024, rising by $5.3 billion, the report said. Its total expenditure of $56.8 billion exceeded the combined spending of all other nuclear-armed states, it said.

China spent $12.5 billion, followed by Britain at $10.4 billion, which was an increase of $2.2 billion, ICAN said.

It said the other nuclear-armed states were France, India, Israel, North Korea, Pakistan and Russia.

“In terms of kind of the increase in spending in the UK and France, I think we certainly have seen, at least in the rhetoric of political leaders, a reference to the ongoing war in Ukraine, to the tensions, and that could be playing a role,” Alicia Sanders-Zakre, a policy and research coordinator at ICAN, told reporters at a briefing in Geneva.

Britain and other allies in NATO now regard Russia as the main security threat to Europe and some have rolled out plans to devote a higher percentage of GDP to defence spending.

However, Sanders-Zakre said the increase in nuclear expenditure has been more driven by the costs of servicing long-term contracts and the growing expense of developing nuclear delivery systems than by current security concerns.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Financial Services Headlines at 12 AM ET: Chime Financial Is the Latest IPO to Soar in Debut | Why ...
data.description:
Chime Financial Is the Latest IPO to Soar in Debut

Chime Financial's stock jumped in its stock-market debut Thursday, the latest sign the U.S. initial public offering market is awakening.

----

Why Larry Fink Sees BlackRock Shares, Income Doubling by 2030

The world's largest asset manager plans to raise $400 billion from clients by 2030 for its funds that invest in private equity, private credit, real estate, infrastructure and other so-called alternatives to public stocks and bonds.

----

Norway Wealth Fund Finds TD Bank an 'Unacceptable Risk'

Norway's $1.9 trillion sovereign wealth fund has placed Toronto-Dominion Bank under observation for a period of four years over financial crime concerns.

----

Coatue Management's Philippe Laffont Sees 2025 as 'Year of the Privates'

The investor points to artificial intelligence and other disruptive technologies as key areas for future investment.

----

Professional Investors Worry They're Falling Behind in Alts, Survey Finds

More than 90% of institutional investors and advisors say they plan to increase their exposure to private markets in the next two years.

----

More Financial Advisers Are Outsourcing Investment Decisions

Assets following model portfolios rose to a record $8 trillion in April.

----

Chime IPO: Stock Soars in Nasdaq Debut, Valuing It at Nearly $14 Billion

Chime, a digital bank popular with younger customers, is the latest tech firm to go public. The stock will begin trading Thursday under the ticker symbol CHYM.

----

The Great ETF Fee War Is Over. You Can Still Get Burned.

Last year, the average asset-weighted fee for index funds stood at 0.11%, essentially flat with 2023. A decade ago, the average fee was 0.19%.

----

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on pay platforms, Deutsche Bank, NatWest and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

----

PwC Overhauls U.S. Advisory Arm, Boosts Hiring to Offer More Industry-Specific Services

PricewaterhouseCoopers is overhauling the structure of its U.S. advisory business and hiring for thousands of roles as it sees recovering demand for such services.

----

World Bank Sees U.S. Growth Rate Halving as Tariffs Slow Global Economy

The world's largest economy is expected to grow by just 1.4% in 2025, a sharp deceleration from the 2.8% expansion recorded in 2024.

----

Moody's Sounds Alarm on Private Funds for Individuals

Chasing retail money could pose risks to investors, private funds and the financial system, Moody's said.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 12 AM ET: This crucial corner of the energy market is at high risk from an all-out war between Israel and Iran - and it's not oil | Oil ...
data.description:
This crucial corner of the energy market is at high risk from an all-out war between Israel and Iran - and it's not oil

An all-out war between Israel and Iran would pose a serious threat to eastern Mediterranean natural-gas fields - and risk spilling into Europe's liquefied natural gas supply.

----

Oil Stages Timid Pullback As Geopolitical Risk Remains

Oil futures held onto most of yesterday's gains despite some early profit-taking amid high Middle East risk perception.

----

U.S. Natural Gas Storage Improves, but Long-Term Challenges Remain

U.S. natural-gas storage levels have improved since a colder-than-expected winter drained inventories, but rapid growth in liquefied natural-gas exports is causing concerns that the demand will result in higher prices later this year - and beyond.

----

Sunnova Bankruptcy Loan and Sales Approved, Hurdles Remain

The rooftop-solar installation company received approval for an interim loan and to sell some assets to finance its bankruptcy proceedings

----

U.S. Natural Gas Mixed With Focus on Weather Outlook

U.S. natural gas futures put in a mixed performance, supported by a warmer weather outlook and a pickup in LNG feedgas flows. Nymex gas for July delivery settled down, while August and September contracts edged up.

----

Iran Says It Will Open Secret New Enrichment Site After U.N. Atomic Agency Censure

Tehran's move casts a fresh shadow over nuclear talks with Washington, while President Trump says an Israeli strike "could very well happen."

----

Saudi Arabia Is Grabbing Oil-Market Share, but It Can Open the Tap Only So Far

Riyadh wants to squeeze U.S. frackers, although an all-out price war would be costly.

----

EPA to Repeal Air-Pollution Standards on Power Plants

The proposals would roll back Biden-era climate rules and Obama-era mercury limits.

----

How Amazon's Nuclear Deal Could Solve a Touchy Political Problem

The data-center agreement with Talen Energy could help ease fears of rising electricity prices for homeowners.

----

U.S. Moves to Withdraw Some Middle East Personnel as Iran Tensions Rise

The State Department ordered all nonessential staff to leave the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad and authorized the departure of nonessential staff and families from Bahrain and Kuwait.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insights on oil futures, BP, Medco Energi Internasional, and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

----

U.S. Crude-Oil Stockpiles Down More Than Expected

U.S. crude-oil inventories fell last week by 3.6 million barrels, surpassing an expected decline of 1.6 million-barrels.

----

NRG Energy Used This Accounting Trick to Make Its Earnings Look Less Volatile

The company's interpretation of accounting rules allows it to mask swings in its derivative contracts.

----

Kimmeridge Cements Plan to Build Integrated Natural-Gas Company

Backing from sovereign-wealth investor Mubadala helped open the door for a U.S. wellhead-to-water business.

data.title: Oil India Shares Rise 3.0%, Oil & Natural Gas Shares Gain 1.6%
ICRA to Acquire 100% Stake in Fintellix India for $26 million
data.description:
Ratings and research firm ICRA has agreed to acquire a 100% stake in Fintellix India for $26 million, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Fintellix is a product-led company specializing in risk, supervisory, and data analytics solutions, offered on its data platform.

The acquisition will boost ICRA Group's credit risk assessment and monitoring tool portfolio by incorporating risk reporting capabilities into its current offerings, the filing said.

data.title: Dimerix Meets Around 70% of Recruitment Target for Phase Three Kidney Disease Trial; Shares Down 5%
data.description:
Dimerix said that a total of 200 adult patients were randomized and dosed in its Action3 phase three clinical trial in patients with focal segmental glomerulosclerosis, representing around 70% of the total number of patients to be dosed in the two-year study, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The firm is targeting a total recruitment of around 286 adult patients. Full recruitment of patients into the trial is on track for the second half.

The trial is seeking to evaluate the efficacy and safety of the firm's DMX200 treatment candidate in patients with focal segmental glomerulosclerosis, a kidney disease, the filing said. The participants are receiving a stable dose of an angiotensin II receptor blocker, and once this dose is stable, patients will be randomized to receive either DMX200 or a placebo.

The trial is designed to capture evidence of kidney function and high levels of protein in the urine, which will be used as evidence to support marketing approval. The company has activated over 190 clinical sites in 22 countries.

The company's shares fell over 5% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Crédit Agricole Unit Buys Majority Stake in France's Comwatt
data.description:
Crédit Agricole's subsidiary Crédit Agricole Transitions & Énergies purchased a majority stake in Comwatt, a France-based supplier of photovoltaic equipment.

The banking group said Thursday the deal will not have a significant impact on its common equity tier 1 ratio.

data.title: Western Region Gold to Acquire Xinjiang Meisheng Mining for 1.66 Billion Yuan; Shares Soar 10%
data.description:
Western Region Gold plans to acquire Xinjiang Meisheng Mining for 1.66 billion yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

Xinjiang Meisheng will be bought from its controlling shareholder, Xinjiang Non-ferrous Metals Industry (Group).

Xinjiang Meisheng has a mine that is still under development, with trial operations beginning in late May, the Chinese gold smelting company announced.

The company's shares soared 10% in recent trade.

data.title: Guoyuan Securities Offering Up to 1.5 Billion Yuan Bonds
data.description:
Guoyuan Securities is offering up to 1.5 billion yuan in three-year, unsecured bonds.

Proceeds from the bonds, due June 17, 2028, will be used for repaying medium- and long-term bonds, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Western Securities serves as underwriter.

Shares of the securities brokerage were down nearly 1% in recent trade.
